title: USD Composition Arbitrator Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: Only the AOUSD core specification at aousd/specifications-public v1.0.1 is normative for this ledger. This ledger covers the central composition-arbitrator boundary and not OpenUSD PCP implementation details.

status_values.implemented: In scope for the composition-arbitrator contract and expected from generated targets that claim this node.
status_values.optional_supported: Supported for an optional spec input or capability, but not required for baseline compliance.
status_values.partial: A bounded subset is included and later contracts must extend it.
status_values.deferred: Specified behavior intentionally not included in this first composition-arbitrator contract.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to another subsystem or future skill.

coverage_rules: The composition arbitrator owns final LIVERPS arbitration across available graph opinion sources.
Stage population consumes the composed NamespaceSource facade and does not evaluate composition arcs.

A generated target must not claim recursive composition, payload loading, full value resolution, schema fallbacks, value clips, or implementation-specific prototype query APIs from this first bounded contract.

obligations: Build prim and property opinion indexes from the root/local layer stack plus available composition arc opinions.
Expose composed prim and property inputs through the namespace-source boundary.
Aggregate composition diagnostics without aborting unrelated path composition.
items.deferred_reason: The first boundary is a central arbitration contract over bounded sources; recursive arc discovery and complete value composition are deferred.

obligations: Order available opinions strong-to-weak as local, inherits, variants, relocates, references, payloads, specializes.
Keep graph construction order separate from opinion strength order.
Do not let individual arc providers or stage population own final family ordering.
Preserve local layer-stack opinion order supplied by the layer-stack namespace source.
Treat stronger layers in the same layer stack as stronger opinions.
Do not reopen or re-resolve sublayers in the composition arbitrator.
items.deferred_reason: Full local layer-stack strength still depends on bounded source coverage; asset resolver and value-resolution details remain outside the composition arbitrator.

obligations: Represent namespace depth, authored/implied status, and source-visible sibling order when supplied by an arc source.
Use those values in the CompositionOpinion strength key before exposing composed fields.
Declare partial composition when an input provider cannot supply implied arcs or recursive sibling arc data.
items.deferred_reason: The current bounded arc providers do not yet supply every implied or recursive arc needed for full same-family ordering.

obligations: Require variant selections to be computed from source-visible opinions after other arc opinions are available.
Insert selected variant opinions at variant LIVERPS strength in the final stack.
Keep selected variant paths out of stage population's composed scene paths.
Keep specializes opinions globally weaker than local, inherits, variants, relocates, references, and loaded payloads.
Do not infer specializes strength from the graph position of the specializes provider.
Permit variants to observe specializes for selection while still inserting selected variant opinions stronger than specializes.
Expose relationship targetPaths and attribute connectionPaths from consumed arc-source opinions in composed scene coordinates.
Prune target or connection path elements that cannot be mapped through the consumed source's namespace mapping.
Perform remapping before stage population or backend queries observe the composed property fields.
Expose instance-filtered descendant prim and property inputs that exclude local descendant specs under an instanceable prim.
Include supported non-local composition arc opinions authored on the instanceable prim, plus recursive opinions from the source prim of such arcs.
Expose supported composition-arc provenance for stage-population shared representation matching.
Expose a composed namespace-source facade for stage population.
Require stage population to preserve composed child/property ordering and avoid composition arc evaluation.
Declare source capabilities so stage queries cannot overclaim missing composition features.
Expose enough strong-to-weak property opinion provenance for a value-resolution provider to consume.
Do not claim value resolution from the composition arbitrator's flattened population-facing fields.
Route metadata, attribute, relationship, connection, clip, retiming, and fallback value queries through the usd-value-resolution provider.
Report payload_loading=absent when no loaded payload opinion source is supplied.
Do not include loaded payload opinions when the payload source is omitted, even if payload metadata and payload layers are present in the adapter input.
Aggregate diagnostics emitted by a supplied payload opinion source.

spec_refs: aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:3935 composition input whether payload arcs are considered
aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:4261 payloads loaded or ignored by composition input
aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:5208 population mask may provide payload flag
obligations: Composition arbitrator reports payload_loading=partial when a loaded-payload opinion source is supplied.
Composition arbitrator reports payload_loading=absent when no payload opinion source is supplied.
Runtime stage open maps the adapter payloadInclusion=omit scenario to no supplied payload opinion source.
Runtime stage open maps the adapter payloadInclusion=include scenario to opened direct payload assets supplied as payload opinions.
items.deferred_reason: Fine-grained payload load masks, unloaded-payload prim state, and load/unload mutation remain future optional payload-loading work.
